cards.front: What problem do header guards solve?
cards.back: Header guards prevent a header file from being #included multiple times in the same compilation unit, which would cause duplicate definition errors.

cards.text: Header guards use {{c1::#ifndef}}, {{c2::#define}}, and {{c3::#endif}} preprocessor directives to prevent multiple inclusion.

cards.question: Write proper header guards for a file named math.h
cards.explanation: The guard macro name typically matches the filename in uppercase, with underscores instead of dots.

cards.back: First inclusion: ifndef succeeds, define sets the macro, content included. Subsequent inclusions: ifndef fails (macro already defined), content skipped.

cards.text: The header guard macro name should be {{c1::unique}} to avoid conflicts. Typically use {{c2::FILENAME_H}} or {{c3::PROJECT_FILENAME_H}}.

cards.back: #pragma once is a non-standard but widely supported alternative to header guards that ensures a header is included only once per compilation.

cards.question: Rewrite this header guard using #pragma once
cards.explanation: #pragma once is simpler and achieves the same result as traditional header guards.

cards.front: What are the advantages of #pragma once over traditional header guards?
cards.back:
Advantages:
  • Simpler, less code
  • No risk of macro name collisions
  • Potentially faster compilation
Disadvantage: Not part of C++ standard (but widely supported).

cards.explanation:
Guidelines for header guards:
  • ALWAYS use header guards in every header file
  • Use unique macro names (include project/path if needed)
  • Wrap entire header content (except comments before guard)
  • Or use #pragma once for simplicity
  • Never define guard macros elsewhere in code

cards.question: What's wrong with this header guard?
cards.code:
#ifndef MATH_H
int add(int a, int b);
#endif
#define MATH_H
cards.answer: #define must come right after #ifndef, before the content. This doesn't properly guard the header.
cards.explanation: The #define needs to be placed after #ifndef to set the guard macro before any content.
